<!--
  This is the upgrade plan template generated by the "Generate Upgrade Plan" agent.
  SESSION_ID should be replaced with the actual session id.

  ## PLANNING RULES

  !!! DON'T REMOVE THIS COMMENT BLOCK BEFORE FINAL PLAN IS GENERATED AS IT CONTAINS IMPORTANT INSTRUCTIONS.

  ### Strategy
  - **Incremental upgrades**: Use intermediates to avoid large jumps breaking builds
  - **Minimal changes**: Only upgrade dependencies essential for compatibility
  - **Risk-first**: Handle EOL/challenging deps early in isolated steps
  - **Necessary/Meaningful steps only**: Each step MUST change code/config. NO steps for pure analysis/validation. Merge small related changes.

  ### Intermediate Version Strategy
  Use intermediates when direct upgrade risks breaking builds. Good intermediate has:
  - **Stability**: Stable LTS release with production track record
  - **Compatibility bridge**: Bridges compatibility between current and target versions

  ### Mandatory Steps
  - **Step 1 (MANDATORY)**: Setup Environment - Install all JDKs/build tools marked `<TO_BE_INSTALLED>`
  - **Step 2 (MANDATORY)**: Setup Baseline - Run compile/test with current JDK, document results.
  - **Steps 3-N**: Upgrade steps - dependency order, high-risk early, isolated breaking changes
  - **Final step (MANDATORY)**: Final Validation - verify all goals met, all TODOs resolved, 100% tests pass (if tests enabled in Options)

  ### Verification Expectations
  - **Steps 1-N (Setup/Upgrade)**: Focus on COMPILATION SUCCESS. Tests may fail during intermediate steps.
  - **Final Validation**: COMPILATION SUCCESS + 100% TEST PASS (if tests enabled in Options)
  ### Efficiency (IMPORTANT)
  - **Targeted reads**: Use `grep` over full file reads; read specific sections, not entire files. Template files are large - only read the sections you need.
  - **Quiet commands**: Use `-q`, `--quiet` for build/test commands when appropriate
  - **Progressive writes**: Update plan.md incrementally, not at end
-->

### Technology Stack

<!--
  Table of core dependencies and their compatibility with upgrade goals.
  IMPORTANT: Analyze ALL modules in multi-module projects, not just the root module.
  Only include: direct dependencies + those critical for upgrade compatibility.
  CRITICAL: Identify and clearly flag EOL (End of Life) dependencies - these pose security risks and must be upgraded.

  Columns:
  - Technology/Dependency: Name of the dependency (mark EOL dependencies with "⚠️ EOL" suffix)
  - Current: Version currently in use
  - Min Compatible Version: Minimum version that works with upgrade goals (or N/A if replaced)
  - Why Incompatible: Explanation of incompatibility, or "-" if already compatible. For EOL deps, mention security/support concerns.

  IMPORTANT: Include build tools (Maven/Gradle), wrappers, and key build plugins in this table.
  Build tools and plugins are upgrade-critical even though they are not runtime dependencies.

  SAMPLE:
  | Technology/Dependency    | Current | Min Compatible | Why Incompatible                               |
  | ------------------------ | ------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
  | Java                     | 8       | 21             | User requested                                 |
  | Spring Boot              | 2.5.0   | 3.2.0          | User requested                                 |
  | Spring Framework         | 5.3.x   | 6.1.x          | Spring Boot 3.2 requires Spring Framework 6.1+ |
  | Maven (wrapper)          | 3.6.3   | 3.9.0          | Maven 3.6.x does not support Java 21           |
  | maven-compiler-plugin    | 3.8.1   | 3.11.0         | Older versions cannot compile Java 21 bytecode |
  | maven-surefire-plugin    | 2.22.2  | 3.1.0          | Older versions may fail with Java 17+ module system |
  | Hibernate                | 5.4.x   | 6.1.x          | Spring Boot 3.x requires Hibernate 6+          |
  | javax.servlet ⚠️ EOL     | 4.0     | N/A            | Replaced by jakarta.servlet in Spring Boot 3.x; javax namespace EOL |
  | Log4j ⚠️ EOL             | 1.2.17  | N/A            | EOL since 2015, critical security vulnerabilities; replace with Logback/Log4j2 |
  | DWR ⚠️ EOL             | 3.0.1.rc  | N/A            | EOL since 2017, no longer maintained; consider replacing with modern alternatives |
  | Lombok                   | 1.18.20 | 1.18.20        | -                                              |
-->

### Derived Upgrades

- Upgrade `demo/pom.xml` `maven.compiler.source/target` from `11` → `21` (Java 21 target/source)
- Upgrade `demo/pom.xml` maven-compiler-plugin to 3.11.0 explicitly (Java 21 bytecode requires 3.11+)
- Upgrade `ecommerce/pom.xml` `maven.compiler.source/target` property and plugin `<source>/<target>` from `11` → `21`
- Upgrade `ecommerce/pom.xml` maven-compiler-plugin from `3.8.1` → `3.11.0` (required for Java 21 bytecode)
- Update `knightsoftheroundtable/pom.xml` `java.version` property from `17` → `21` (Spring Boot 3.1.x supports Java 21)

## Upgrade Steps

### Step 1: Setup Environment

- **Rationale**: All required JDKs (11, 17, 21) and Maven 3.9.12 are already installed. No installation needed.
- **Changes to Make**:
  - [x] Verify JDK 21.0.1 at `/Users/dodero/.sdkman/candidates/java/21.0.1-tem`
  - [x] Verify Maven 3.9.12 at `/opt/homebrew/Cellar/maven/3.9.12/bin`
- **Verification**: `#appmod-list-jdks` — SKIP (already verified)

---

### Step 2: Setup Baseline

- **Rationale**: Establish pre-upgrade compile and test results for all three modules.
- **Changes to Make**:
  - [ ] Run compile + tests for `demo` with JDK 11
  - [ ] Run compile + tests for `ecommerce` with JDK 11
  - [ ] Run compile + tests for `knightsoftheroundtable` with JDK 17
- **Verification**:
  - Command: `JAVA_HOME=<jdk11> mvn clean test -f code/demo/pom.xml` etc.
  - Expected: Document pass/fail counts as baseline acceptance criteria

---

### Step 3: Upgrade `demo` to Java 21

- **Rationale**: Update compiler source/target from 11 → 21 and add explicit maven-compiler-plugin 3.11.0.
- **Changes to Make**:
  - [ ] Set `maven.compiler.source` and `maven.compiler.target` to `21` in `demo/pom.xml`
  - [ ] Add explicit maven-compiler-plugin 3.11.0 declaration
- **Verification**:
  - Command: `JAVA_HOME=<jdk21> mvn clean test -f code/demo/pom.xml`
  - JDK: `/Users/dodero/.sdkman/candidates/java/21.0.1-tem`
  - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS, all tests pass

---

### Step 4: Upgrade `ecommerce` to Java 21

- **Rationale**: Update compiler source/target from 11 → 21 in both properties and plugin configuration. Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin 3.8.1 → 3.11.0.
- **Changes to Make**:
  - [ ] Set `maven.compiler.source`, `maven.compiler.target` properties to `21`
  - [ ] Update `<source>` and `<target>` inside maven-compiler-plugin config to `21`
  - [ ] Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin version from `3.8.1` → `3.11.0`
- **Verification**:
  - Command: `JAVA_HOME=<jdk21> mvn clean test -f code/ecommerce/pom.xml`
  - JDK: `/Users/dodero/.sdkman/candidates/java/21.0.1-tem`
  - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS, all tests pass

---

### Step 5: Upgrade `knightsoftheroundtable` to Java 21

- **Rationale**: Update the `java.version` property from 17 → 21. Spring Boot 3.1.0 supports Java 21.
- **Changes to Make**:
  - [ ] Set `java.version` property to `21` in `knightsoftheroundtable/pom.xml`
- **Verification**:
  - Command: `JAVA_HOME=<jdk21> mvn clean test -f code/knightsoftheroundtable/pom.xml`
  - JDK: `/Users/dodero/.sdkman/candidates/java/21.0.1-tem`
  - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS, all tests pass

---

### Step 6: Final Validation

- **Rationale**: Confirm all three projects compile and pass all tests with Java 21. Satisfy Upgrade Success Criteria.
- **Changes to Make**:
  - [ ] Verify all pom.xml files target Java 21
  - [ ] Resolve any remaining TODOs
  - [ ] Clean rebuild all three modules with JDK 21
  - [ ] Run full test suite on all modules, fix any failures (iterative loop)
- **Verification**:
  - Command: `JAVA_HOME=<jdk21> mvn clean test -f code/demo/pom.xml && JAVA_HOME=<jdk21> mvn clean test -f code/ecommerce/pom.xml && JAVA_HOME=<jdk21> mvn clean test -f code/knightsoftheroundtable/pom.xml`
  - JDK: `/Users/dodero/.sdkman/candidates/java/21.0.1-tem`
  - Expected: Compilation SUCCESS + 100% tests pass across all modules

## Key Challenges

- **JUnit 3.8.1 in `demo`**
  - **Challenge**: Extremely old test framework; reflection-based runner. May interact unexpectedly under Java 21 strong encapsulation.
  - **Strategy**: Compile and run tests; if reflective access issues arise, add `--add-opens` JVM args or replace test runner.

- **Spring Boot 3.1.0 + Java 21**
  - **Challenge**: Spring Boot 3.1.0 added official Java 21 support retroactively. Some internal reflection-based operations may trigger warnings.
  - **Strategy**: Compile and test directly. Upgrade to 3.1.x latest patch if issues arise.

- **Multiple independent modules**
  - **Challenge**: Three separate Maven modules must each be compiled and tested individually; no parent/multi-module POM.
  - **Strategy**: Upgrade and validate each module independently then run final validation on all three.

## Plan Review

- All three Maven projects under `gii-dss/code/` are covered.
- Java 21.0.1 is already installed — Step 1 (Setup Environment) verified and skipped.
- Maven 3.9.12 is compatible with Java 21 — no build tool upgrade needed.
- Spring Boot 3.1.0 supports Java 21 — no Spring Boot upgrade needed.
- JUnit versions (3.8.1, 4.13.2) are compatible with Java 21.
- `dao-example` (untracked, stashed) is excluded as it has no committed state.
- No unfixable limitations identified.
